마태복음 17:1.content: After six days, Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them up into a high mountain by themselves.

마태복음 17:2.content: He was changed before them. His face shone like the sun, and his garments became as white as the light.

마태복음 17:3.content: Behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them talking with him.

마태복음 17:4.content: Peter answered, and said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you want, let’s make three tents here: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”

마태복음 17:5.content: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them. Behold, a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to him.”

마태복음 17:6.content: When the disciples heard it, they fell on their faces, and were very afraid.

마태복음 17:7.content: Jesus came and touched them and said, “Get up, and don’t be afraid.”

마태복음 17:8.content: Lifting up their eyes, they saw no one, except Jesus alone.

마태복음 17:9.content: As they were coming down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, “Don’t tell anyone what you saw, until the Son of Man has risen from the dead.”

마태복음 17:10.content: His disciples asked him, saying, “Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

마태복음 17:11.content: Jesus answered them, “Elijah indeed comes first, and will restore all things,

마태복음 17:12.content: but I tell you that Elijah has come already, and they didn’t recognize him, but did to him whatever they wanted to. Even so the Son of Man will also suffer by them.”

마태복음 17:13.content: Then the disciples understood that he spoke to them of John the Baptizer.

마태복음 17:14.content: When they came to the multitude, a man came to him, kneeling down to him, and saying,

마태복음 17:15.content: “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is epileptic, and suffers grievously; for he often falls into the fire, and often into the water.

마태복음 17:16.content: So I brought him to your disciples, and they could not cure him.”

마태복음 17:17.content: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation! How long will I be with you? How long will I bear with you? Bring him here to me.”

마태복음 17:18.content: Jesus rebuked him, the demon went out of him, and the boy was cured from that hour.

마태복음 17:19.content: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately, and said, “Why weren’t we able to cast it out?”

마태복음 17:20.content: He said to them, “Because of your unbelief. For most certainly I tell you, if you have faith as a grain of mustard seed, you will tell this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.

마태복음 17:21.content: But this kind doesn’t go out except by prayer and fasting.”

마태복음 17:22.content: While they were staying in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is about to be delivered up into the hands of men,

마태복음 17:23.content: and they will kill him, and the third day he will be raised up.” They were exceedingly sorry.

마태복음 17:24.content: When they had come to Capernaum, those who collected the didrachma coins came to Peter, and said, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the didrachma?”

마태복음 17:25.content: He said, “Yes.” When he came into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth receive toll or tribute? From their children, or from strangers?”

마태복음 17:26.content: Peter said to him, “From strangers.” Jesus said to him, “Therefore the children are exempt.

마태복음 17:27.content: But, lest we cause them to stumble, go to the sea, cast a hook, and take up the first fish that comes up. When you have opened its mouth, you will find a stater coin. Take that, and give it to them for me and you.”
